title: The Shattered Peak
description: A narrative-driven survival horror created during the Digital Bros Game Academy course. Play as a child lost in the mountains, searching for his family while being pursued by something unknown.
longDescription:
Created during the **Digital Bros Game Academy** course, the project was centered around the theme of **3D Survival**. The game focuses heavily on narrative and storytelling, drawing inspiration from *What Remains of Edith Finch*. Our aim was to evoke a strong emotional response from the player as they experience the unfolding plot.

In the game, you play as a child living in the mountains, searching for his lost family while uncovering secrets that were never meant to be revealed. He must learn to survive in a harsh environment while being pursued by something (or someone) unknown.

## My Role

As **Lead Programmer**, I specialized in **Gameplay and AI**, and implemented the majority of the game's core features. The team consisted of fewer than 10 people in total.

This project taught me a lot about **Unreal Engine 5 and C++**, particularly how to write robust, optimized, clean, maintainable, and well-documented code, while also balancing the use of Blueprints. I researched the best approaches and methodologies to ensure high-quality results. I also developed each feature with a **design-oriented** mindset, ensuring that designers had the flexibility to customize features to **bring their visions to life**.

Additionally, this project gave me valuable experience in collaborating with other "departments", mirroring the structure of a real game studio, which was one of the team's and course's primary goals.

## Creature AI Behavior

I developed an AI-controlled creature inspired by the "Slender" game, implementing complex behaviors and interactions with the player. The creature spawns at a variable distance from the player based on the danger level, which changes according to the player's location and playtime.

The spawn area is dynamically adjusted based on the areas the player is in and the objects near the spawn area, always trying to play "hide and seek" to increase the anxiety it provides to the player. The creature can spawn in the area between two concentric circles around the player and will despawn if the player moves beyond a maximum distance or after a set time calculated at random.

Additionally, the creature triggers various effects — sounds and visual distortions — when the player looks at it for a certain period, adding to the eerie atmosphere. These effects smoothly adjust based on proximity and danger level, ensuring a seamless and immersive experience.

## Audio Manager

The Audio Manager consists of a component in the GameMode that handles all the SFX present in the game. It controls all the logic for deciding when to play audio cues, their volume, and other related parameters, based on different game conditions (such as the creature's presence, its distance from the player, and many other factors).

This system ensures that audio effects are dynamically responsive to the in-game situation, adding another layer of immersion by changing the auditory experience based on the player's actions and surroundings. Designers are able to customize every detail of the sound to be played — whether it should be spatial or not, its location, if it should loop, and many other features that were thought out together with the designers to give them the power to express themselves.

## Other Mechanics

**HuntZone System** — Multiple trigger zones placed throughout the map define areas where the creature can spawn and interact with the player. These zones are managed by a subsystem class which keeps track of active zones and ensures the creature spawns only when the player enters these areas. Each zone has a specific danger level that influences spawn mechanics and behavior.

**Flashlight** — The player's main tool for seeing in the dark and hostile environment. In certain areas and under specific conditions, it helps reveal important parts of the story. However, only the most observant players will be able to discover all the hidden secrets.

**Teddy Bear** — The key item to fight off the Creature in later stages when its presence is strongest. Since the main character is a child, we wanted his most powerful tool to be his trusted companion. When used, the teddy bear repels the Creature and lights up the surroundings, but only for a short time, giving the player a brief moment of relief and a chance to find safety.

In addition to these mechanics, I also contributed to:
- **Player Features** — Movement, sprinting, and stamina management
- **Subsystems** — Various underlying systems to support gameplay functionality
- **UI** — In-game widgets with a diegetic UI style
- **VFX and Camera Effects** — Visual effects and camera interactions
- **Version Control** — Co-managed the Git repository using Git Flow branching strategy

## Personal Considerations

Working on this project was an amazing experience. I loved getting to know so many talented people who shared my interests and dedication. Balancing this project with my regular job was a real challenge, but I'm so glad I took it on. I can't even count how much I've learned during this time, and I'm excited to keep learning more every day.

I won't lie — there were some tough moments, especially trying to meet all the deadlines with limited time, while still delivering results we could be proud of. Some of us gave up all our free time to make this happen, and I feel really lucky to have worked with such dedicated people through all those hours. I look forward to continuing to work with them even after this course is over.
techHighlights: AI creature with dynamic spawn system and danger levels
Audio Manager with designer-customizable SFX parameters
HuntZone subsystem with trigger volumes and trace channels
Enemy logic with C++ state machine and State Tree
role: Lead Programmer, Gameplay & AI - Team of ~8
